protocol.name: T1DM Beta Cell Recovery Protocol v1.0

phases.name: Baseline & Setup
phases.goal: Establish baseline measurements, set up monitoring, build medical team

actions.details: Find an endocrinologist willing to monitor an experimental supplement protocol. Bring the 1-page protocol summary (included below). You need: (1) someone who will order labs, (2) someone who will adjust insulin as needed, (3) someone who won't panic at the word 'fasting' in T1DM. If your current endo won't do it, find one who will.

Dexcom G7 or Libre 3. If insurance covers it, use insurance. If not: Libre 3 reader + sensors = ~$75/month without insurance. CGM is NON-NEGOTIABLE for this protocol. You cannot fast safely without it.
Keto-Mojo GK+ meter (~$50) + ketone strips (~$1 each). You need this for every fasting day. Urine strips are NOT acceptable (they measure acetoacetate, not BHB; 6-12hr lag time).
Order ALL supplements now so they arrive before Week 1. See SHOPPING LIST section for exact products.
Fasting draw (12hr overnight fast). This is the most important single blood draw of the entire protocol. Everything is measured against this.
labs: C-peptide, stimulated/MMTT ($150 -- requires clinic visit, Boost drink, timed draws at 0/30/60/90/120 min)

actions.details: Echocardiogram: establishes baseline ejection fraction (EF). Screens for occult CVB myocarditis (EF <50% = flag). Abdominal ultrasound: visualize pancreas (atrophy? calcification?). These are one-time baseline tests.

Wear CGM for at least 7 days before starting protocol. This gives you: (1) baseline glucose patterns, (2) time in range, (3) overnight patterns, (4) post-meal patterns. Save screenshots. This is your 'before' data.

Bring: (1) baseline labs, (2) CGM data, (3) protocol summary, (4) safety profile document, (5) your supplement list. Key asks: Will you monitor me? Will you order quarterly labs? Will you adjust my insulin if C-peptide rises? Can you prescribe fluoxetine 10mg (or refer to PCP/psychiatrist)? Can we discuss semaglutide at month 4 if things look good?

phases.name: Foundation -- Reduce Destruction
phases.goal: Clear virus (fluoxetine), reduce inflammation (vitamin D, butyrate), establish fasting practice. Target: D drops by ~50%.

model_prediction.dB_dt: still declining but much slower (-4.3e-4 vs -1.2e-3 /day)

actions.details: Take in the morning with breakfast. Half dose for 2 weeks to minimize side effects. Common side effects (week 1-2): nausea, headache, insomnia, anxiety increase, GI upset. These typically resolve by week 3-4. If severe nausea: take with food. If insomnia: take in AM, not PM. IMPORTANT: Fluoxetine can mask hypoglycemia symptoms (tremor, anxiety) by ~15-20%. Raise CGM low alarm to 80 mg/dL (from typical 70).

Take with a fat-containing meal (improves absorption 50%). Morning or evening, same time daily. If baseline 25-OH-D was <20 ng/mL: start with 5000-6000 IU for first 2 months, then drop to 4000 IU. Target blood level: 50-70 ng/mL. If >100 ng/mL on any check: reduce dose.

Take with meals (morning + evening). Butyrate smells bad -- enteric-coated capsules avoid the smell and GI issues. Start with 1 capsule for 3 days, then add the second. Common early effect: mild GI discomfort, loose stool. Usually resolves in 1 week. Mechanism: butyrate upregulates FOXP3 expression -> expands regulatory T cells -> suppresses autoimmune attack.

Full therapeutic dose. If side effects from 10mg were severe, stay at 10mg for another 2 weeks before increasing. 20mg is the target dose for CVB 2C ATPase inhibition. At steady state (4-6 weeks), brain concentration reaches ~15x plasma = ~4.5 uM, well above the 1.0 uM IC50.

THIS IS THE MOST IMPORTANT SAFETY EVENT IN THE PROTOCOL.

Preparation:
  - Eat your last meal at 6 PM
  - Reduce basal insulin to 80% of normal dose
  - No bolus insulin during fast
  - CGM on, ketone meter ready
  - Glucose tabs, juice box within arm's reach
  - Tell someone you are fasting

During fast:
  - Check blood ketones at hours 0, 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24
  - Watch CGM continuously
  - Drink water, black coffee, plain tea -- no calories
  - Light activity only (walking OK, no gym)
  - Log everything: BG, ketones, how you feel

ABORT if:
  - Blood glucose < 60 mg/dL (or <70 with symptoms)
  - Blood BHB > 3.0 mM
  - Nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain
  - Feeling confused or unable to self-monitor

Break fast at 6 PM next day with a normal meal.
Take full bolus insulin for the meal.

EXPECTED: BHB peaks 1.0-1.5 mM at 80% basal. This is ideal -- enough to suppress NLRP3 inflammasome, not enough for DKA.
Quick safety check. 3 tests only.

The Fasting Mimicking Diet is the ENGINE of regeneration.

Option A: ProLon kit ($199/cycle, pre-packaged, easiest)
Option B: DIY FMD (~$30/cycle):
  Day 1: ~1100 kcal (11% protein, 46% fat, 43% carbs)
  Days 2-5: ~800 kcal (9% protein, 44% fat, 47% carbs)
  Focus: nuts, olives, vegetables, small amounts of grain
  NO animal protein, NO sugar, NO dairy

Insulin adjustments during FMD:
  - Basal: 80-85% of normal (75% if on semaglutide later)
  - Bolus: 40-50% of normal (small FMD meals)
  - Check ketones 2x/day
  - CGM alarm at 80 mg/dL

REFEEDING (Days 6-8): THIS IS WHERE REGENERATION HAPPENS
  - Day 6: light meals, normal insulin
  - Day 7-8: normal eating, slightly larger portions
  - mTOR reactivates, IGF-1 rebounds
  - Ngn3+ pancreatic progenitors activate
  - NEW BETA CELLS ARE BORN in this window
  - Each cycle: ~0.3% beta cell mass gain

This is the FIRST EFFICACY CHECK. The question: is anything happening?
C-peptide is the primary endpoint. Even a small rise (0.12 -> 0.15) is signal.

Review Month 3 labs with endocrinologist.

IF C-peptide rose (even slightly): Protocol is working. Continue. Move to Phase 2.
IF C-peptide flat: Protocol may need more time. Continue Phase 1 for 3 more months. Consider: Was fluoxetine taken consistently? Were FMD cycles completed? Is vitamin D at target? Fix compliance before adding more components.
IF C-peptide dropped: Concerning but not fatal. Check autoantibodies. Consider adding GABA early (move Phase 2 components forward).
IF ALT > 3x ULN: Reduce fluoxetine to 10mg. Recheck in 2 weeks.
IF hs-CRP unchanged or rising: Investigate other sources of inflammation.

phases.name: Amplify -- Boost Regeneration
phases.goal: Add GABA (transdifferentiation + anti-inflammatory) and semaglutide (proliferation + sensitivity). Target: R begins approaching D.

actions.details:
GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) has TWO mechanisms:
  1. Anti-inflammatory: reduces Teff expansion by ~20%
  2. Transdifferentiation: activates GABA-A on alpha cells -> Pax4 -> alpha-to-beta identity switch

Start 250mg with each meal (3x/day = 750mg total). Can increase to 500mg 3x/day (1500mg) after 2 weeks if tolerated. Side effects: mild drowsiness (especially first week), slight dizziness. If drowsy: take larger dose at bedtime, smaller doses at meals. GABA does NOT cross the blood-brain barrier well in adults, so central sedation is minimal. GI effects are the main route.

Semaglutide (Ozempic) is a GLP-1 receptor agonist. Off-label for T1DM but increasingly studied (ADJUNCT ONE and TWO trials).

What to tell your doctor:
  'I'd like to try low-dose semaglutide as an adjunct to my insulin. The evidence from ADJUNCT trials shows it can reduce insulin dose by 20-30% in T1DM, improve time-in-range, and reduce HbA1c. I understand the hypoglycemia risk and I have CGM monitoring. I'd like to start at 0.25mg weekly and titrate slowly.'

If physician agrees:
  - Start 0.25mg subcutaneous weekly
  - Proactively reduce basal insulin by 10% when starting
  - Watch CGM closely for lows in the first 2 weeks
  - Common side effects: nausea (50% of patients), reduced appetite

If physician declines:
  The protocol works without semaglutide. It provides ~25% R boost and 30% sensitivity improvement, but GABA + FMD are the main drivers. Semaglutide is Tier 3 (premium). Skip it and continue.

After 4 weeks at 0.25mg, increase to 0.5mg if tolerated.
  - Reduce basal insulin by another 10% (total 20% reduction from baseline)
  - Adjust fasting protocol: basal at 70% (not 80%) during fasts
  - Monitor weight: semaglutide causes weight loss. If BMI drops below 20,   discuss with doctor.
  - DO NOT go above 0.5mg. This is not for weight loss --   we want the beta cell and immune effects, not max GLP-1 stimulation.
One 5-day FMD per month. Schedule it the same week each month. The refeeding window (days 6-8) is cumulative -- each cycle adds ~0.3% beta cell mass. After 6 cycles, ~2-3% gain.
The major assessment. This is where we expect to see signal.
Repeat echo if baseline showed any abnormality, or if troponin was elevated. If baseline was normal and troponin normal: skip (save money).

Review all Month 6 data with endocrinologist.

OUTCOME A: C-peptide rising (any amount)
  -> CELEBRATE. The inequality is shifting. Continue to Phase 3.
  -> If stimulated C-peptide > 0.4 nmol/L: you are responding strongly.

OUTCOME B: C-peptide flat but hs-CRP dropped
  -> The anti-inflammatory arm is working but regeneration hasn't kicked in yet.
  -> Add GABA 1500mg/day if not already at full dose.
  -> Continue 3 more months before reassessing.

OUTCOME C: C-peptide flat, hs-CRP flat, autoantibodies unchanged
  -> Consider: is the protocol being followed consistently?
  -> Consider: teplizumab discussion with specialist (see Phase 3).
  -> Do NOT stop -- 6 months may not be enough for a 67-year T1DM patient.
  -> The model predicts inequality reversal at month 8-10.

OUTCOME D: C-peptide dropped
  -> Uncommon. Check for concurrent illness, infection, severe stress.
  -> Repeat in 1 month. Single drop is not failure.

phases.name: Sustain -- The Inequality Reverses
phases.goal: All components running. R overtakes D around month 8-10. Beta cell mass begins GROWING. Begin insulin dose reduction if C-peptide rising.

actions.details:
Fluoxetine 20mg daily
Vitamin D3 4000 IU daily
Sodium butyrate 300mg 2x/day
GABA 250-500mg 3x/day
Semaglutide 0.5mg weekly (if started)
Monthly 5-day FMD cycle

This is the grind. Nothing changes except the numbers. The model says the inequality reverses around month 8-10. You won't feel it. The C-peptide will show it.

actions.action: IF C-peptide rising: begin insulin dose reduction per milestone ladder
actions.details:
C-PEPTIDE MILESTONE LADDER:

0.20 nmol/L (FIRST SIGNAL): No insulin change. Just celebrate.
0.40 nmol/L (EARLY RESPONSE): Reduce bolus by 25%.
0.80 nmol/L (MODERATE RECOVERY): Reduce basal 30%, bolus 50%. DANGER ZONE.
1.20 nmol/L (STRONG RECOVERY): Reduce basal 50%, bolus 75%.
1.80 nmol/L (NEAR INDEPENDENCE): Safety minimum basal only. Trial discontinue bolus.
2.50 nmol/L (INDEPENDENCE): Trial complete discontinuation.

NEVER reduce insulin without CGM data and C-peptide confirmation.
NEVER skip more than one step at a time.
ALWAYS keep insulin pen loaded and accessible.

Teplizumab (Tzield) is an anti-CD3 monoclonal antibody.
FDA-approved for delaying T1DM onset (Herold 2019 NEJM).
Off-label for established T1DM but biologically rational.

Requires specialist (academic endocrinologist or immunologist).
Single 14-day IV infusion course.
Cost: ~$13,000 per course (without insurance).
Side effects: cytokine release syndrome (CRS, flu-like), lymphopenia, rash.

DO NOT fast during teplizumab course.
Resume FMD 4 weeks after completing infusion.

This is Tier 3+. Most patients should try the full Phase 1-2 protocol for 9-12 months before considering teplizumab.
Interim check. Primary focus: C-peptide trajectory.
One-year milestone. Comprehensive re-evaluation.

actions.action: DECISION POINT #3 -- Continue, Modify, or Celebrate
actions.details:
Review 12-month data.

RESPONDING (C-peptide up, insulin dose down):
  -> Continue all components. Move to Phase 4 (Maintenance).
  -> If C-peptide > 0.8 nmol/L: you are on track for independence.
  -> Projected timeline: insulin independence 18-36 months.

PARTIAL RESPONSE (C-peptide up slightly, insulin unchanged):
  -> The inequality reversed but slowly. Continue for another 6-12 months.
  -> Consider semaglutide if not started. Consider teplizumab.

NO RESPONSE (C-peptide flat after 12 months):
  -> The 20-35% of patients who may not respond at 3 years.
  -> Options: (1) continue anyway (no harm, supplements are safe), (2) stop protocol, (3) try teplizumab, (4) wait for next-gen interventions.
  -> Even no response = you are healthier from vitamin D, reduced inflammation, improved gut health, and better glucose control from fasting discipline.

phases.name: Maintenance -- Consolidate Gains
phases.goal: If responding: continue protocol, reduce insulin per milestones, reduce monitoring frequency. If near independence: trial discontinuation.

actions.details:
If responding:
  - Fluoxetine 20mg daily (continue for full 24 months minimum)
  - Vitamin D3 4000 IU daily (indefinite)
  - Butyrate 300mg 2x/day (continue)
  - GABA 250-500mg 3x/day (continue)
  - FMD: can reduce to every 6-8 weeks if C-peptide steadily rising
  - Semaglutide: continue if well-tolerated

If not responding after 12 months:
  - Can stop FMD (it was worth trying)
  - Continue fluoxetine + supplements (still beneficial)
  - Consider stopping semaglutide (expensive if not helping)

At 18 months, the CVB clearance model predicts all 8 compartments cleared.
Consider tapering fluoxetine IF:
  - C-peptide has been rising for 6+ months
  - Autoantibodies declining or stable
  - hs-CRP normal

Taper: 20mg -> 10mg for 2 weeks -> 10mg every other day for 2 weeks -> stop.
NEVER stop fluoxetine abruptly (discontinuation syndrome: dizziness, brain zaps, irritability, flu-like symptoms).

If C-peptide is still flat: continue fluoxetine. No harm in longer duration.

Two-year mark. The model's expected case:
  B ~ 0.28, C-peptide ~ 0.75 nmol/L, insulin ~ 7 U/day

SUCCESS (C-peptide > 0.8 nmol/L, insulin reduced >50%):
  -> Continue maintenance: vitamin D + butyrate + GABA
  -> Quarterly FMD if desired
  -> Semi-annual labs (C-peptide + HbA1c)
  -> Continue insulin reduction per milestone ladder
  -> You are on track for independence at 30-36 months

PARTIAL SUCCESS (C-peptide 0.3-0.8, insulin reduced somewhat):
  -> Continue protocol. You are responding, just slowly.
  -> Consider extending to 36 months.
  -> Even if you never reach independence, every 0.1 nmol/L of C-peptide reduces complications, reduces hypo frequency, and improves quality of life.

NO RESPONSE (C-peptide unchanged after 24 months):
  -> Reasonable to stop active protocol components.
  -> Continue vitamin D and butyrate (general health benefits).
  -> The supplements were safe and the attempt was rational.
  -> You learned your autoimmune phenotype: likely not CVB-driven, or   beta cell reserve was truly exhausted.

items.notes: Generic fluoxetine. GoodRx price ~$4-10/month. Do NOT use brand Prozac ($300+). Ask pharmacist for generic.

items.example_product: NatureWise Vitamin D3 4000 IU or NOW Vitamin D3 5000 IU
items.notes: Cholecalciferol (D3), not ergocalciferol (D2). Take with fat. 5000 IU products are fine -- take 5000 IU on alternating days with 4000 IU or just take 5000 IU daily and adjust if levels go above 70 ng/mL.
MUST be enteric-coated or you will taste/smell butyrate (rancid butter). Calcium/magnesium butyrate also acceptable. Tributyrin (e.g., CoreBiome) is a prodrug alternative.
ProLon: $199 first kit, then $149/mo subscription. DIY option: nuts, olives, vegetable soups = ~$30-50/cycle. See FMD protocol details above for exact macros.

items.example_product: NOW GABA 750mg capsules (take 1 capsule 2x/day = 1500mg) or Thorne PharmaGABA 100mg (chewable, more bioavailable but more expensive)
items.notes: Regular GABA powder capsules are fine. PharmaGABA (fermented) may have slightly better absorption but costs 3x more. Start low (250mg 1x/day) and increase over 2 weeks.

Ozempic with insurance: $25-50 copay. Without insurance: ~$900-1000/month. Compounding pharmacies: $150-300/month (check local). Canadian pharmacy (with valid Rx): ~$250-350/month. If cost prohibitive: SKIP. The protocol works without it.
With insurance: usually covered for T1DM ($0-50 copay). Without insurance: Libre 3 ~$75/month, Dexcom ~$150/month. Libre 3 is the budget option and perfectly adequate.
Keto-Mojo GK+ kit: ~$50 (meter + 10 strips). Additional strips: ~$1/strip, need ~30/month during fasting.

tests: Requires clinic: Boost drink + timed draws 0/30/60/90/120 min

MONTH 1 (Safety check).priority: Fluoxetine liver safety + vitamin D compliance

MONTH 6 (Comprehensive -- KEY decision p.priority: FULL panel. This determines Phase 2 -> Phase 3 transition.

MONTH 18 (Durability).priority: Confirm sustained response. Fluoxetine taper decision.

MONTH 24 (Final protocol assessment).priority: COMPREHENSIVE. Two-year verdict. Compare to every prior result.

severity_levels.symptoms: Shakiness, sweating, hunger, fast heartbeat, anxiety
action: Eat 15g fast carbs: 4 glucose tabs, 4oz juice, or 1 tbsp honey
Wait 15 minutes
Recheck blood glucose
If still <70: repeat 15g carbs
Once >70: eat a snack with protein + fat
Log the event. Identify cause (too much insulin? missed meal? exercise?)

severity_levels.symptoms: Confusion, difficulty speaking, blurred vision, weakness
action: Eat 30g fast carbs immediately
If unable to eat safely: glucagon nasal spray (Baqsimi) or injection
Call someone to stay with you
Recheck in 15 minutes
If no improvement: CALL 911
After recovery: eat a full meal
Report to endocrinologist. Review insulin doses.

severity_levels.symptoms: Seizure, loss of consciousness, unable to self-treat
action: CALL 911 IMMEDIATELY
If glucagon available: bystander should administer
Do NOT put food in mouth of unconscious person
Place on side (recovery position)
This is a medical emergency

dka.title: DKA SYMPTOMS (Diabetic Ketoacidosis)
dka.triggers: Most likely during fasting, illness, missed insulin, or pump failure
action: STOP FASTING immediately -- eat carbs
Check blood glucose AND blood ketones
If BHB > 3.0 mM with symptoms: GO TO ER
If BHB > 5.0 mM: CALL 911 -- this IS DKA
Take correction insulin dose (50% of normal correction factor)
Drink water (dehydration accelerates DKA)
Do NOT exercise (worsens ketosis)
Do NOT take exogenous BHB supplements
ER will give IV fluids + insulin drip

chest_pain.title: CHEST PAIN / CARDIAC SYMPTOMS
chest_pain.context: CVB can cause myocarditis/pericarditis. Protocol includes cardiac monitoring.
warning_signs: Chest pain (especially sharp, worse with breathing or lying down = pericarditis)
Shortness of breath at rest or with minimal exertion
action: STOP all exercise immediately
If severe chest pain or difficulty breathing: CALL 911
If mild and intermittent: urgent cardiology appointment (within 24-48h)
Troponin and BNP blood test ASAP
ECG and echocardiogram
DO NOT fast until cardiac evaluation is complete
Continue fluoxetine and supplements (these are not cardiac-risky)
Report to protocol-monitoring endocrinologist

severe_side_effects.title: SEVERE SIDE EFFECTS

stop_immediately_if: Severe agitation, tremor, fever, diarrhea (serotonin syndrome -- RARE)
Suicidal thoughts (rare in adults, report immediately)
Severe abdominal pain that won't stop (pancreatitis risk)
Severe vomiting unable to keep fluids down >24h
reduce_dose_if: Weight loss >5% in first month (too fast for T1DM patient)

severe_side_effects.general_rule: When in doubt, contact your endocrinologist. If they are unavailable and symptoms are severe, go to the ER. Bring your medication list and tell them you are on an experimental supplement protocol for T1DM beta cell recovery.
